verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient has non-small cell lung cancer.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
This can be found in the OpenEMR patient chart (Onc Patient 7), tab CLINICALNOTES, note type Progress Notes, row 39, column D, which says, "stage I non-small cell lung cancer." 

This also requires synthesizing the statement from the script, which says, "I’m sorry the lung cancer you developed several years ago has returned. It is the same type as before." Putting both together means this patient has non-small cell lung cancer.

verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient's cancer is metastatic.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: This can be found in the attached patient script. "Now, that same cancer has spread to some lymph nodes and a small spot in the opposite lung." This indicates metastasis.

verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient's cancer has an HER2 mutation.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: This can be found in the attached patient lab results. "HER2 Positive."

verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient has already received systemic therapy.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
This can be found in the attached patient script. "You completed platinum‑based chemotherapy with immunotherapy." 
Both of these are forms of systemic therapy. "Chemotherapy is usually systemic, meaning the chemotherapy drug travels throughout your entire body." https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/treatments/16859-chemotherapy

verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient's non-small cell lung cancer is non-squamous.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
This is provided in the attached patient script. "We know that it is non-squamous, but we need more information."

From OpenEMR patient chart (Onc Patient 7): tab CLINICALNOTES, note type Progress Notes, row 39, column D, which says, "stage I non-small cell lung cancer." 
This also requires synthesizing the statement from the script, which says, "I’m sorry the lung cancer you developed several years ago has returned. It is the same type as before." Putting both together means this patient has non-small cell lung cancer.

verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient wants to avoid further deterioration in her respiratory function during the next treatment.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: This can be found in the attached patient script. "I’m also worried about the spread of my lung cancer and how it will impact my breathing, so I want to avoid medications that could make my breathing worse."

verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient wants to avoid reducing her white blood cell count during the next treatment.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: This can be found in the attached patient script. "I also didn’t like the increased risk of infection with the last round of treatment, so I don’t want a medication that takes away my white blood cells again."

verifier_values.criteria: States that the most appropriate next treatment option is zongertinib.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
The patient has been diagnosed with non-squamous, metastatic, non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) and has already completed prior chemotherapy (platinum-based) or immunotherapy. The patient's cancer is positive for a HER2-activating mutation (based on the attached lab results), and the patient has expressed a desire to avoid medications that could worsen breathing or reduce white blood cell count (based on the attached script). 
From the OpenEMR patient chart (Onc Patient 7): tab CLINICALNOTES, note type Progress Notes, row 39, column D

The patient meets all indications for initiating zongertinib. "Zongertinib is a kinase inhibitor indicated for the treatment of adult patients with unresectable or metastatic non-squamous non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) whose tumors have HER2 (ERBB2) tyrosine kinase domain activating mutations who have received prior systemic therapy." 

Based on the side effect profile of zongertinib, this medication does not cause worsening respiratory function or reduce white blood cell counts. 
https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/drugsatfda_docs/label/2025/219042s000lbl.pdf

verifier_values.criteria: States that zongertinib is dosed once daily.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: This is the frequency of zongertinib. "The recommended dosage of HERNEXEOS is based on body weight, < 90 kg: 120 mg, ≥ 90 kg: 180 mgTake HERNEXEOS orally once daily." https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/drugsatfda_docs/label/2025/219042s000lbl.pdf

verifier_values.criteria: States that zongertinib is dosed orally.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: This is the route of administration of zongertinib. "The recommended dosage of HERNEXEOS is based on body weight, < 90 kg: 120 mg, ≥ 90 kg: 180 mgTake HERNEXEOS orally once daily." https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/drugsatfda_docs/label/2025/219042s000lbl.pdf

verifier_values.criteria: States that the patient weighs 50.8 kg (acceptable range 50.3 to 51.3).
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: This is the patient's most recent weight according to the chart, "Onc Patient 7": tab, "CLINICALNOTE", note type "Progress Notes", row, "246", column, "D" which states that the patient weighs 50.8 kg.

verifier_values.criteria: States that zongertinib is dosed at 120 mg for this patient.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation:
This is the dosage of zongertinib. "The recommended dosage of HERNEXEOS is based on body weight, < 90 kg: 120 mg, ≥ 90 kg: 180 mgTake HERNEXEOS orally once daily." https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/drugsatfda_docs/label/2025/219042s000lbl.pdf. 
This is the patient's most recent weight according to the chart, "Onc Patient 7": tab, "CLINICALNOTE", note type "Progress Notes", row, "246", column, "D", which states that the patient weighs 50.8 kg. The patient's most recent weight in the chart is 50.8 kg. This is under 90 kg, so the zongertinib dose is 120 mg daily, orally. The patient weighs less than 90 kg; therefore, the zongertinib dose is 120 mg daily.

verifier_values.criteria: States that zongertinib can be reduced to 60 mg if the patient has side effects.
verifier_values.criteria_explanation: This potential dose reduction is indicated by the manufacturer of zongertinib. "Recommended HERNEXEOS Dose Reductions for Adverse Reactions: Current HERNEXEOS Dose First Reduction: 120 mg 60 mg." https://www.accessdata.fda.gov/drugsatfda_docs/label/2025/219042s000lbl.pdf
